name: Ishikawa Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the Sea of Japan and the prefecture's coastal character
purity, snow and the Hakusan mountains

description: A light-blue field charged at the centre with a white circular emblem stylising the katakana イシ (ishi), the first part of Ishikawa.
history: The prefectural symbol was adopted in 1972 along with the flag in white on a light-blue field.

features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the katakana イシ (ishi) arranged into a rounded form evoking the contours of Mount Haku (Hakusan).
